sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 14.53 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.14)

statement.latex: Conjecture:\/ Let$G$be a profinite group such that the set of solutions of the equation$x^n=1$has positive Haar measure. Then$G$has an open subgroup$H$and an element$t$such that all elements of the coset$tH$have order dividing$n$. This is true in the case$n=2$. It would be interesting to see whether similar results hold for profinite groups in which the set of solutions of some equation has positi...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
